words.definition: Government by the whole population, usually through elected representatives.
words.example: Modern democracy relies on free and fair elections.

words.definition: A state in which supreme power is held by the people and their elected representatives.
words.example: The republic was proclaimed after the monarchy fell.

words.definition: Government by a single hereditary ruler.
A monarchy in which the sovereign's powers are limited by a constitution.
A monarchy where the ruler has unlimited authority.
Government by a small privileged class, often hereditary.
words.example: The Venetian aristocracy governed for centuries.

words.definition: Government by a small group of people.
words.example: Post-Soviet Russia was described as an oligarchy.

words.definition: Government by the wealthy.
words.example: Critics accused the era of plutocracy.

words.definition: Government by a single person with absolute power.
Government by a dictator, typically without free elections.
A system of centralised, dictatorial government requiring complete subservience to the state.
words.example: Totalitarianism seeks to control both public and private life.

words.definition: A form of government characterised by strong central power and limited political freedoms.
Government by religious leaders in the name of a deity.
words.example: Iran is often described as a theocracy.

words.definition: Rule by church officials.
words.example: Medieval Papal States were an ecclesiocracy.

words.definition: A state of society without government or law.
A political theory advocating self-governed societies with no ruling authority.
A political theory advocating class war leading to a society in which all property is publicly owned.
words.example: Marx envisioned communism as the final stage of history.

words.definition: A political and economic theory advocating collective ownership of the means of production.
words.example: Nordic socialism blends welfare with markets.

words.definition: An authoritarian, ultranationalist political ideology.
words.example: Fascism glorifies the nation over the individual.

words.definition: A system in which power is divided between a central authority and constituent political units.
words.example: US federalism reserves specific powers to the states.

words.definition: A union of sovereign states with a weak central government.
words.example: The Articles of Confederation preceded the US Constitution.

words.definition: A state governed as a single unit with a supreme central government.
A system in which the executive derives its legitimacy from the legislature.
words.example: In a parliamentary system, the prime minister must retain the confidence of parliament.

words.definition: A system in which the president is head of government and independent of the legislature.
words.example: The United States uses a presidential system.

words.definition: A military or political group ruling a country after seizing power.
A government in which those in power exploit resources for personal gain.
Government by those selected on the basis of ability or achievement.
words.example: Reforms aimed to build a genuine meritocracy.

words.definition: Government by technical experts.
words.example: Some argue climate policy demands more technocracy.

words.definition: Government by old people.
words.example: Critics called the ruling council a gerontocracy.

words.definition: Government by the worst or least qualified.
words.example: Satirists reached for the word kakistocracy.

words.definition: Government by the mob.
words.example: Plato feared democracy could decay into ochlocracy.

words.definition: Government by property owners or those honoured for their virtue.
Government by the military.
A social system in which women hold primary power.
words.example: Some scholars debate whether historical matriarchies existed.

words.definition: A system in which men hold primary power.
words.example: Feminist theory analyses patriarchy across cultures.

words.definition: A regime whose actions are controlled by an outside power.
words.example: Occupying forces installed a puppet government.

words.definition: A government formed by two or more parties.
words.example: No party won a majority, so a coalition government was formed.

words.definition: A temporary government that manages routine affairs.
words.example: A caretaker government held office until the election.

words.definition: An interim government formed to prepare for a permanent regime.
words.example: The provisional government drafted the new constitution.

words.definition: Rule by a regent during the minority or absence of a monarch.
words.example: The regency ended when the prince came of age.

words.definition: An extensive group of states ruled by a single sovereign or state.
words.example: The Roman Empire endured for centuries.

words.definition: The policy of acquiring political control over another country.
A policy of extending a country's power through diplomacy or force.
The right to vote in political elections.
A direct vote of qualified electors on a matter of public importance.
words.example: A plebiscite decided the border dispute.

words.definition: A general vote by the electorate on a single political question.
words.example: The referendum settled the constitutional issue.

words.definition: A body of fundamental principles by which a state is governed.
Supreme authority within a territory.
A system of government with many appointed officials.
words.example: The bureaucracy processed thousands of applications daily.

words.definition: Government by two independent authorities.
words.example: Sparta famously had a diarchy of two kings.

words.definition: Republic with parliamentary executive.
Republic with a directly elected president.
words.example: The US is a presidential republic.

words.definition: System with both president and PM.
Citizens vote on laws directly.
Citizens elect representatives.
Democracy with civil liberties.
Elections without full civil rights.
words.example: The state slid into illiberal democracy.

words.definition: Republic ruled by a constitution.
words.example: A constitutional republic limits state power.

words.definition: Monarch chosen by election.
words.example: Poland once had an elective monarchy.

words.definition: Throne passes by descent.
Rule by two people.
Rule by three.
Rule by corporations.
Rule by digital elites.
words.example: Netocracy describes a networked power class.

words.definition: Rule by law.
Government where all have equal power.
Utopian equal-rule community.
Flexible, non-bureaucratic organization.
Rule by the workers.
Rule by plantation owners.
Rule by sea power.
words.example: The Athenian thalassocracy dominated the Aegean.

words.definition: Rule by judges.
Interlocking hierarchical power.
Rule through information systems.
Rule by a single ethnic group.
words.example: Critics called the state an ethnocracy.

words.definition: Corrupt oil-based rule.
State ruled by fascist party.
State ruled by a communist party.
State led by socialist principles.
State prioritising national identity.
League of sovereign states.
Union of partially self-governing states.
Transfer of powers to regions.
State ruled by a sultan.
State ruled by an emir.
Islamic religious-political state.
Office and rule of the Pope.
Feudal Japanese military rule.
Sovereign city with its territory.
State subordinate to another.
State protected by a stronger one.
words.example: The islands were a British protectorate.

words.definition: Self-governing former colony.
Control over another state's foreign affairs.
words.example: China claimed suzerainty over the region.

words.definition: Nominally independent but externally controlled.
words.example: The puppet regime followed foreign orders.

words.definition: Period between two reigns.
words.example: The interregnum ended with a coronation.

words.definition: Temporary government after change.
Rulers displaced from home country.
Only one legal party.
Several competing parties.
Central government rules a region directly.
